title: Owned Starlight not Yoga
codeOnlyAnswer: Based only on code and git evidence, the answer should push back on reintroducing Yoga/Yoga-compatible nodes/styles. Current code exposes and implements Starlight as Lynx's layout engine: the standalone C API uses SLNodeRef, SLNodeStyleSet*, SLNodeCalculateLayout, and StarlightMeasureDelegate, and its implementation wraps lynx::starlight::LayoutObject plus LayoutComputedStyle, not Yoga. Git history shows commit fc6e4ec1 titled replace yoga, deleting ReactCommon/yoga/* while adding ReactCommon/starlight/*, plus later Yoga cleanup/removal commits. A new layout feature should be added to Starlight's own style/model/API boundary rather than exporting Yoga semantics. Uncertainty: code/git show Yoga was replaced and removed, but do not fully explain the precise design rationale or reopening condition.
mctsAwareAnswer: The design-aware answer should say no by default: Lynx's current design is an owned Starlight layout backend, not Yoga or a Yoga-compatibility facade. The MCTS node records owned Starlight layout backend identity and a renderer-independent layout scheduling/API boundary; its Yoga alternative is frozen in .alt and was replaced by layout-starlight in commit fc6e4ec1 with the recorded reason: owning the engine lets Lynx encode CSS-native style and measurement contracts directly and removes Yoga as a public semantic option. The feature should be designed as Starlight-native style/algorithm/API work, preserving the standalone/API boundary, not by exposing Yoga-compatible nodes/styles or making Yoga flex behavior public. Reopen Yoga only if new evidence shows the old rejection reason has lapsed.

gradeNotes.codeOnly: Strengths: identifies Starlight implementation, Yoga replacement/removal from git, standalone API/code anchors, and states uncertainty. Misses: precise recorded Yoga rejection reason and reopening condition tied to that reason.
gradeNotes.mctsAware: Covers live MCTS item, Yoga .alt replacement reason, standalone/API boundary, code anchors, and reopening condition tied to recorded reason.
